brixs
  • brixs.Spectra
brixs
  • brixs.Spectra
  • View page source

brixs.Spectra

class brixs.Spectra(*args, **kwargs)

Returns a spectra object.

Parameters:

  • n (int, optional) – array preallocation.

  • data (list or array, optional) – list of spectrum objects.

  • folderpath (string, path object) – folderpath.

  • filepath (str or pathlib.Path, optional) – filepath to read.

How to initialize a Spectra object:

Empty

>>> ss = br.Spectra()

Pre-allocation

>>> ss = br.Spectra(n=10)
>>> ss = br.Spectra(10)

From Spectrum

>>> ss = br.Spectra(s1, s2, ...)
>>> ss = br.Spectra([s1, s2, ...])
>>> ss = br.Spectra(data=[s1, s2, ...])

where s1, s2, … are Spectrum type.

From file

>>> ss = br.Spectra(folderpath=<folderpath>)
>>> ss = br.Spectra(<folderpath>)
>>> ss = br.Spectra(filepath=<filepath>)
>>> ss = br.Spectra(<filepath>)

where filepath must point to a xy-type file, where comments must be marked with # and columns must be separated by , (comma). Folderpath must point to a folderpath populated only with files similar xy-type files.

data

list with Spectrum objects.

Type:

list of Spectrum

folderpath

folderpath associated with data.

Type:

str or pathlib.Path

filepath

filepath associated with data.

Type:

str or pathlib.Path

step

step size for x-coordinates.

Type:

number, read only

length

length of x-coordinates vector.

Type:

number, read only

x

x-coordinates.

Type:

number, read only

monotonicity

monotonicity of the x-array.

Type:

str or None, read only

peaks

each entry represents a parameter of a peak.

Type:

Peaks

Computed (read-only) attributes:
area (list)

area of each spectrum

sum (Spectrum)

sum of every spectrum

average (Spectrum)

average of every spectrum

map (Image)

2D representation of spectra

calculated_shift (list)

calculated x add. factor

calculated_offset (list)

calculated y additive factor

calculated_roll (list)

calculated x roll

calculated_calib (list)

calculated x multiplicative factor

calculated_factor (list)

calculated y multiplicative factor

Write-only attributes:
calib

calls s.set_calib()

shift

calls s.set_shift()

roll

calls s.set_roll()

factor

calls s.set_factor()

offset

calls s.set_offset()

__init__(*args, **kwargs)

Initialize the object instance.

Raises:

AttributeError – if kwargs and args cannot be read.

Methods

__init__(*args, **kwargs)

Initialize the object instance.

align([mode])

Uses calculate_shift and set_shift.

append(*args, **kwargs)

Append spectrum to the spectrum list.

calculate_average(*args, **kwargs)

Returns Spectrum object with the average of all spectra.

calculate_calib([values, mode, deg])

Calculate calibration factor via Spectra.calculate_shift().

calculate_emission_map()

Calculates emission map.

calculate_emission_spectra()

Calculates emission spectra.

calculate_energy_map()

Calculates energy map.

calculate_factor([mode])

Calculate mult.

calculate_map([axis])

Return image representation of spectra.

calculate_momentum_map([energy, ...])

Calculates momentum map.

calculate_offset([mode])

Calculate offsets to align data background.

calculate_roll([mode])

Calculate x-coord.

calculate_shift([mode])

Calculate x-coord.

calculate_sum(*args, **kwargs)

Returns Spectrum object with the sum of all spectra.

calculate_th_map()

Calculates th map.

calculate_y_sum(*args, **kwargs)

Returns a list of the sum of y elements within a range for each spectra.

check_length()

Checks if all spectra has the same length.

check_monotonicity()

Sets monotonicity attribute to 'increasing' or 'decreasing'.

check_same_x([max_error])

Check if spectra have same x-coordinates.

check_step([max_error])

Check step between data points in the x-coordinates.

concatenate()

Return spectrum of concatenate spectra.

copy(*args, **kwargs)

Return a copy of the object with data contained in a range.

copy_peaks_from_spectra()

Copy peaks from each spectrum.

copy_peaks_to_spectra()

Copy peaks to each spectrum

crop([start, stop])

Crop data.

find_peaks([prominence, width, ...])

Find peaks recursively.

fit_peak([asymmetry, method, ranges, verbose])

Fit one peak for all spectra recursively.

fit_peaks([method, ranges])

Fit peaks for all spectra SIMULTANEOUSLY.

fix_monotonicity([mode])

Rearrange x, y such as x array is monotonically increasing or decreasing.

flip()

Flip x axis.

flip_order()

reorder spectra backwards:

floor(*args, **kwargs)

Sets zero value for y-coordinates (shifts data verticaly).

get_by_attr(attr, value[, closest, verbose])

Return spectrum with attr closest to value.

interp([start, stop, num, step, x])

Interpolate data.

load([folderpath, string, verbose])

Load data from text files.

load_from_single_file([filepath, only_data, ...])

load multiple spectra from file.

normalize([mode])

Uses Spectra.calculate_factor() and Spectra.set_factor() to normalize spectra.

plot([ax, offset, shift, factor, calib, ...])

Plot spectra.

polyfit(deg, *args, **kwargs)

Fit data recursively with a polynomial.

remove(idx)

Remove spectrum.

reorder(i1, i2)

reorder spectra.

reorder_by_attr(attr[, decreasing])

reorder spectra based on a attr.

roll_plot([xlim, ylim, vlines, update_function])

flip through spectra (up/down keys), roll spectrum (left/right keys)

roll_plot2([xlim, ylim, vlines, update_function])

[experimental] flip through spectra (up/down keys), roll spectrum (left/right keys).

save([folderpath, prefix, suffix, ...])

Save spectra.

save_all_single_file([filepath, only_data, ...])

Save all Spectra in one single file.

sequential_plot([xlim, ylim, update_function])

plot where you can use arrows to flip through spectra.

set_calib(value)

Apply multiplicative x factor recursively.

set_factor([value])

Apply multiplicative y factor recursively.

set_offset([value])

Apply additive y factor recursively.

set_roll([value])

Roll y data recursively.

set_shift([value])

Shift data recursively.

shift_plot([xlim, ylim, step, vlines, ...])

flip through spectra (up/down keys), shift spectrum (left/right keys)

switch()

Switch x and y axis
